description.p: Word Hunter is a desktop language-learning workspace for reading real texts, saving vocabulary with context, and reviewing words later.
It supports imported text, PDFs, EPUB files, subtitles, public-domain book discovery, local vocabulary storage, spaced repetition, text to speech, and optional offline translation models.

screenshot.caption: Reading view with vocabulary highlights and word details

ul.li: Add 48 original A1-B2 graded readers across 12 learning languages.
Let readers choose and reorder selected-word controls and save grammatical articles.
Improve Pocket word panels, flashcards, progress charts, themes, accessibility, and desktop layouts.
Fix Reader refreshes, stale synchronization updates, review races, selection return, and delayed translations.
Replace course-style claims with 48 original A1-B2 graded readers across all 12 named learning languages.
Provide continuous, progressively leveled stories for reading comprehension and contextual vocabulary.
Correct reader titles and cover metadata throughout the built-in Library catalog.
Add 48 original A1-B2 books across all 12 named learning languages.
Offer lessons, dialogues, practical texts, contextual vocabulary, and practice activities.
Use CEFR-inspired levels for Latin and Ancient Greek.
Keep the Pocket word sheet at a stable usable height in expanded, collapsed, and custom positions.
Let the grammatical Article field be shown, hidden, and reordered with the other selected-word items.
Collapse the long word-panel ordering controls in Reader settings when they are not needed.
Keep background synchronization from rebuilding an unchanged Reader and returning it to the top.
Restore complete frontend release validation after the RC7 TypeScript additions.
Let the Pocket word card travel across most of the screen and keep its chosen height when Smart Suggestion changes.
Make word-card swipes easier and move larger navigation targets closer to the bottom of tall phones.
Add visible grammatical-article editing, suggestions, display, flashcards, search, and import/export support.
Recognize French and Italian apostrophe articles without creating duplicate vocabulary keys.
Prevent stale synchronization snapshots from reverting newly changed word statuses.
Acknowledge backend snapshots only after the frontend accepts the exact revision.
Return Ctrl+Enter to the selected Reader word without falling back to the page start.
Keep delayed SRS grades from overwriting newer status changes.
Extend the Pocket word-sheet drag range and retain intermediate heights between cards.
Tint the complete selected-word card to match New, Learning, Known, or Ignored status.
Keep status controls, outgoing card colors, and loading states synchronized.
Avoid rebuilding release artifacts after a validated draft is published.
Run complete word selection and automatic translation after Reader swipes.
Add a draggable collapsed or expanded Pocket word-panel sheet with an accessible handle.
Animate horizontal card drags with immediate content updates and layered transitions.
Prevent stale translation refreshes and improve gesture release handling.
Keep Familiar desktop themes dark from the first paint and in embedded tools.
Add swipe navigation with directional animation to selected Reader words.
Turn Flashcards into a card-first deck with tap-to-reveal and safe swipe navigation.
Open clipped Pocket heatmaps at the latest weeks in Graphs and Flashcards.
Give each light desktop theme a distinct background and panel surface instead of plain white.
Fix the desktop Edit Book dialog so labels stay compact and fields use the available width.
Test prerelease: migrate the browser runtime to TypeScript with a deterministic generated frontend.
Add configurable visibility and ordering for selected-word panel sections and actions.
Move Pocket word actions higher and improve theme, state, dialog, and drawer reliability.
Fixed Windows saves and startup recovery failing with access denied while synchronizing backup files.
Made Pocket clearly explain when a scanned PDF requires desktop OCR before synchronization.
Fixed the Android Import drawer closing whenever a user tapped one of its controls.
Included the OCR reliability, progress animation, synchronized counters, and sentence correction fixes from the withdrawn 1.0.1 and 1.0.2 builds.
Restored the live PaddleOCR progress animation with a clearer document-scanning indicator.
Changed quick PDF sentence correction to edit and replace only the selected sentence.
Added automatic CPU recovery when accelerated PDF OCR fails at runtime.
Kept library word counters stable while synchronized book text refreshes in the background.
Updated release workflows to supported GitHub Actions runtimes and corrected Rust toolchain setup.
Established a new compatibility baseline after breaking storage and synchronization changes.
Added durable per-record local storage, safer recovery, and deterministic cross-device conflict handling.
Added guided Syncthing setup on desktop and staged folder synchronization on Android.
Refreshed Windows, Android, and x86_64 Flatpak packaging, validation, and third-party license notices.
Added a managed cloud sync coordinator with a Flatpak Google Drive connector through bundled rclone; cloud pulls are staged and validated before merge, and the MVP does not delete remote files.
Recovered interrupted Google Drive authorization by clearing partial app-owned rclone remotes and showing a retryable sign-in state.
Retried transient Google Drive quota and rate-limit failures while preparing the cloud sync folder.
Kept the visible Google Drive connector status stable across local/background sync snapshots instead of falling back to not connected after a successful connection.
Removed the separate Discover language selector; book discovery now follows the active learning profile language on desktop and Android.
Fixed Discover source selection so switching to Wikipedia, Wikinews, or Wikisource is preserved across Flatpak/background sync snapshots.
Improved the Linux/Flatpak GTK titlebar workaround so close and maximize buttons keep receiving clicks in normal window mode.
Fixed Flatpak sync folder selection so the chosen folder is stored only after a successful sync and uses XDG config/data locations.
Fixed Flatpak/Linux OCR GPU platform detection for more reliable startup diagnostics.
Bundled WebGPU/Vulkan OCR acceleration with a safe CPU fallback, PDFium, and PaddleOCR models for scanned PDFs on Linux.
Refreshed Windows packaging so installer and portable builds include the required GNU runtime DLLs.
Added Linux Flatpak packaging with AppStream metadata and local install helpers.
Improved PDF reading with page-background overlays, selectable words, and a clean text mode.
Improved Android Pocket PDF rendering with zooming, horizontal panning, and text-layer spacing fixes.
Hardened local-first storage, backup, recovery, and sync compatibility paths.
